rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad area (Fitzroy, Collingwood, South Yarra); center avg ~$2,500 but furnished non-central ~AUD 1,800-2,200 (~$1,200-1,500)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; Australian energy costs moderate; internet ~AUD 80/mo

groceries.note: home cooking; Woolworths/Coles/Aldi; AUD $356/mo reported monthly avg

diningOut.note: Melbourne cafe culture; excellent coffee scene; lunch ~AUD 15-20, dinner AUD 30-55

transit.note: Myki monthly ~AUD 160 (~$105); good tram network in inner city

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly from AUD 75 (budget) to AUD $667 (premium); mid estimate ~AUD 280 (~$190)

simData.note: SIM-only plan (Belong/Amaysim/Boost); unlimited data ~AUD 35-45/mo; competitive market

misc.note: gym (~AUD 50), toiletries, entertainment, arts/culture scene, beach trips buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. Melbourne is Southern Hemisphere — summer Dec-Feb. Medium-term furnished lets fairly stable.

evidence.claim: Melbourne digital nomad monthly budget range
evidence.quote: Living in Melbourne as a digital nomad means budgeting between $2,500 and $3,500 per month for rent, food, leisure, transport and internet access
Rent for a 1-bedroom in the center averages $2,500/month
Expect to spend about $356 monthly on groceries
Public transport is priced at around $121 per month
